nativeRange.habitat: Coastal bays, salt marshes, seagrass beds, estuaries, mangroves, and around sponges and floating Sargassum. Depth range 0–100 m, typically shallow intertidal and neritic zones.

diet.notes: Has been documented accepting frozen mysis shrimp as a staple; captive-bred specimens tend to adapt more readily than wild-caught. Also takes live or frozen amphipods, enriched adult brine shrimp, and small crustaceans. Feeding 2–3 times daily is generally advised; juveniles have been reported to consume thousands of nauplii per day. Slow feeders that tend to be outcompeted by faster-moving tankmates.

summary: Peaceful Western Atlantic seahorse from seagrass beds and coastal estuaries. Generally advised to keep as mated pairs in a species-only or pipefish-only tank with ample hitching posts and multiple small daily feedings. Advanced care.
careNotes: Hippocampus erectus (Lined Seahorse / Erectus Seahorse) is generally considered one of the more manageable seahorse species for experienced aquarists, though it tends to demand dedicated husbandry. A species-only or pipefish-only aquarium is strongly advised — faster-moving tankmates have been known to outcompete seahorses for food, leading to malnutrition. A minimum of 30 gallons (113.6 L) for a pair is commonly cited, with a tall tank (at least 45 cm / 18 inches high) preferred to allow natural swimming and courtship behavior. Water flow should be kept low to moderate; strong flow has been reported to stress seahorses and increase risk of bacterial infections. Provide ample hitching posts: gorgonians, branching corals, artificial anchors, and macroalgae. Temperature 21–25.5°C (70–78°F); staying at the lower end of this range tends to be advised as elevated temperatures appear to increase susceptibility to Vibrio bacterial infections. pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12. IUCN status: Vulnerable (VU); CITES Appendix II — captive-bred specimens are strongly preferred over wild-caught. Care level disagreement noted: LiveAquaria rates care as 'Difficult'; AlgaeBarn and some hobbyist sources describe the species as 'moderate to intermediate' among seahorses. 'Advanced' chosen as the average. Lifespan disagreement: FishBase wild data shows max age of 1 year (likely underrepresented due to limited field observation), while aquarium hobby sources consistently report 4–5 years in good captive conditions; some exceptional reports of 8–10 years for well-managed tank-raised lineages (Mustangs/Sunbursts). min=1, max=5 used as averaged estimate. Temperature cross-verification: LiveAquaria range 68–78°F (20.0–25.6°C); AlgaeBarn and Wikipedia suggest 72–77°F (22.2–25.0°C) as optimal; FishBase wild range extends 10–27°C. Aquarium source averages: min ≈ 21°C, max ≈ 25.5°C used.
breedingNotes: Ovoviviparous; males brood eggs in a ventral external pouch. Gestation has been reported at approximately 14–21 days depending on temperature. Females have been observed depositing up to 600 eggs per brood; fry numbers typically range from 50–400. Sexual maturity tends to be reached within nine months. Pairs have been known to form monogamous bonds and perform daily greeting dances. Captive breeding is achievable and has been practiced commercially across many generations; fry require live zooplankton (baby brine shrimp nauplii, copepods) and very frequent feeding.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Lined Seahorse (Captive-Bred) product page. Primary source for care parameters, tank requirements, diet, and compatibility. Manifest listed the seahorses collection page; actual species page navigated directly.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (max 17.8 cm SL confirmed), temperature range (10–27°C wild), taxonomy (Syngnathidae / Syngnathiformes), native range (Western Atlantic, Canada to Brazil), and diet (small crustaceans). Disagreement on lifespan: FishBase reports max age 1 year from wild data, likely an underrepresentation; aquarium sources report 4–5 years captive. Averaged min=1, max=5 years.

additional.name: AlgaeBarn
additional.notes: Third source for care parameters and husbandry. Confirmed temperature 72–77°F (22–25°C), SG 1.020–1.025, pH 8.1–8.4, multiple daily feedings, species-tank preference. Care level described as moderate-to-intermediate, which averaged with LiveAquaria 'Difficult' yields 'advanced'.

additional.name: Wikipedia (Lined seahorse)
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (12–17 cm typical, max 19 cm), native range (Nova Scotia to Venezuela/Brazil), taxonomy, habitat, and lifespan (1–4 years wild, 4 years captive). Used for range and native-range cross-verification.

fish.breedingNotes: Male broods eggs in ventral pouch; gestation 14–21 days; brood size 50–400 fry. Fry require live zooplankton and very frequent feeding. Captive breeding is achievable in dedicated species tanks across many generations.
